commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And their king shall go into captivity, he and his princes together, says the LORD. Melchom, the god or idol of the Ammonites, otherwise called Moloch, and Melech; which, in Hebrew, signifies a king, or Melchom their king. (Challoner) He assumed the title of "their king "Judges xi. , and Jeremias xlix. (Haydock) Blind people, who could not see the vanity of such impotent gods. (Calmet) Both he. Septuagint, "and their priests.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: Melchom: The god or idol of the Ammonites, otherwise called Moloch, and Melech: which in Hebrew signifies a king, and Melchom their king.
